steps.vulnerability_name: Reject alg:none tokens — remove PlainJWT acceptance in validateJwtToken
steps.description: The catch block for ParseException attempts to parse the token as a PlainJWT and returns true unconditionally, meaning any unsigned token is accepted. Remove this branch so the method returns false for unparseable or unsigned tokens.

steps.vulnerability_name: Block alg:none in workshop JWT decode — reject tokens before claims extraction
steps.description: workshop/utils/jwt.py decodes JWTs with verify_signature=False. Even though the identity service already validated the token, a defense-in-depth check should reject any token whose header declares alg:none before local decoding.
steps.search_code:
if response_status_code == status.HTTP_200_OK:
                    decoded = jwt.decode(token, options={"verify_signature": False})

verification.description: Forge an alg:none token for an existing user and attempt to access the identity dashboard — must be rejected with 401
Verify that the identity token verify endpoint rejects an alg:none token
verification.curl: NONE_TOKEN=$(python3 -c "import base64,json; h=base64.urlsafe_b64encode(json.dumps({'alg':'none','typ':'JWT'}).encode()).rstrip(b'=').decode(); p=base64.urlsafe_b64encode(json.dumps({'sub':'user@example.com'}).encode()).rstrip(b'=').decode(); print(f'{h}.{p}.')"); curl -s -w '\nHTTP_STATUS:%{http_code}' -X POST http://167.99.136.89:8888/identity/api/auth/verify -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -d "{\"token\":\"$NONE_TOKEN\"}"
